target.name: Thalassa Robotics
target.founder: Vatsal Varma, Arjun Prabhakar, Bryan Tan

data.narrative:
Thalassa Robotics is a seed-stage company operating in ocean and maritime robotics. The comparable universe consists of five private peers: Standard Subsea, Clippership, NetworkOcean, Albacore, and Odin Dynamics. No public companies in this subsector provide direct market benchmarks.

The absence of applicable valuation methods—no revenue multiples, venture capital method inputs, or precedent transaction data were provided—limits quantitative analysis. Without specific financial metrics from peer companies, revenue figures for Thalassa, or market sizing data, traditional triangulation approaches cannot be deployed. The private comp set alone, without disclosed valuations or financial performance, does not yield actionable valuation guidance.

This analysis cannot produce a supported valuation range without additional data: (1) revenue or ARR figures from at least three comparable private companies, (2) enterprise value or last-round valuation for those peers, (3) Thalassa's own revenue run rate or customer metrics, and (4) a defined enterprise value multiple range for the ocean robotics segment. Seed-stage companies in frontier maritime tech typically command valuations tied to team credentials, IP position, and near-term customer traction rather than comparable multiples. Investors should focus due diligence on the founding team's subsea domain expertise, prototype maturity, and letters of intent from maritime operators before establishing valuation.
